title: Python, Tcl, Tkinter, Threads, and the Event Loop · Behavior Case: Runtime Evidence
summary: The interface creates multiple roots, blocks callbacks with work, calls widgets from an arbitrary thread, and exits with scheduled callbacks still alive. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the runtime evidence increment in single-root event-loop lifecycle contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Record Python, Tcl, Tk, operating system, display, scaling, and startup or shutdown evidence without overgeneralizing.
Explain tkinter thread routing and keep UI mutation on the owning event-loop path.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 8233 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to model a single-root cooperative event loop with bounded callbacks and deliberate shutdown; all graph claims need an independent witness; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. A defensible solution applies Runtime Evidence, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to single-root event-loop lifecycle contract.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-runtime-tk-event-loop-5
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-runtime-tk-event-loop-5.
# Case 9036: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def worked_maze_runtime_tk_event_loop_5(roots: int = 1, pending_callbacks: int = 0, longest_callback_ms: int = 7, ui_thread_owned: bool = True, clean_shutdown: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "gridworked5",
        "topology9036",
        "search5dekrow",
        "eventworked59036",
        "access9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if roots != 1:
        return False, roots, "root-lifecycle"
    if pending_callbacks < 0:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "callback-count"
    if longest_callback_ms < 0 or longest_callback_ms > 16:
        return False, longest_callback_ms, "blocking-callback"
    if not ui_thread_owned or not clean_shutdown:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "runtime-ownership"
    assert roots == 1 and pending_callbacks == 0
    return True, longest_callback_ms, "cooperative-runtime"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Build Runtime Evidence evidence · Theory Runtime Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-runtime-evidence-ykjevp" that uses case 8702 to model a single-root cooperative event loop with bounded callbacks and deliberate shutdown. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-runtime-evidence-ykjevp
# Competency: maze-runtime-evidence.
# Case 8702: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def evidence_maze_runtime_evidence_ykjevp(roots: int = 1, pending_callbacks: int = 0, longest_callback_ms: int = 7, ui_thread_owned: bool = True, clean_shutdown: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ykjevp = "ykjevp-pvejky-8702"
    assert evidence_variant_ykjevp
    evidence_axes_ykjevp = (
        "gridykjevp",
        "topology8702",
        "searchpvejky",
        "eventykjevp8702",
        "access8702ykjevp",
        "recoverypvejky8702",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ykjevp)) == 6
    if roots != 1:
        return False, roots, "root-lifecycle"
    if pending_callbacks < 0:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "callback-count"
    if longest_callback_ms < 0 or longest_callback_ms > 16:
        return False, longest_callback_ms, "blocking-callback"
    if not ui_thread_owned or not clean_shutdown:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "runtime-ownership"
    assert roots == 1 and pending_callbacks == 0
    return True, longest_callback_ms, "cooperative-runtime"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record Python, Tcl, Tk, operating system, display, scaling, and startup or shutdown evidence without overgeneralizing.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Runtime Evidence model · Theory Runtime Evidence, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Runtime Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Runtime Evidence model · Theory Runtime Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 1043 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to model a single-root cooperative event loop with bounded callbacks and deliberate shutdown; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Record Python, Tcl, Tk, operating system, display, scaling, and startup or shutdown evidence without overgeneralizing.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Runtime Evidence model · Theory Runtime Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: One successful launch proves every platform configuration.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-runtime-evidence-ykjevp(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_runtime_evidence_ykjevp\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?roots != 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?pending_callbacks < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?longest_callback_ms < 0 or longest_callback_ms > 16)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not ui_thread_owned or not clean_shutdown)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
